steps.title: Use the starting height
steps.content: The ball starts at a height of $24$ feet, so the exponential model must begin with $24$.

steps.title: Use the bounce multiplier
steps.content: After each bounce, the maximum height is $80\%$ of the previous maximum height. That means the multiplier is $0.80$ each time the ball hits the ground.

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content: After $n$ hits, the model is $$h(n)=24(0.80)^n$$The answer is B.
